introSection: Kong is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo, California, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened as Hangman at Opryland USA on May 1, 1995. It replaced the Tin Lizzies, which closed at the end of 1994. It only operated until 1997, when it was dismantled and sold due to the closure of Opryland USA.

Premier Parks bought the ride as part of a 13 ride package from the defunct park in November 1997. The ride was reported to be rebuilt and relocated to Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in Thorntown, Indiana, USA, but instead subsequently reopened at The New Marine World Theme Park in Vallejo, California, USA in May 1998 as Kong.

Alongside Boomerang Coast to Coaster, it was one of the first roller coasters at the park.

On August 5, 2016, Six Flags announced that Kong would be one of two rides to receive a virtual reality upgrade for Fright Fest, with the other one being Demon at Six Flags Great America. The ride would show a film called Rage of the Gargoyles and it was being billed as "the world's first fully immersive gaming experience". Kong would also receive some refurbished track.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Kong is a Suspended Looping Coaster installation with the 689m standard layout. Following the chain lift hill is a roll over inversion, a banked turn, a sidewinder, and a double in-line twist.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Red track and orange supports. The ride used to feature Orange track and dark green supports, with white track and green supports at Opryland

sections.title: External links
sections.content:
* Kong on the parks website
 * Hangman on the parks website

fullText:
Kong is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo, California, USA.

The ride originally opened as Hangman at Opryland USA on May 1, 1995. It replaced the Tin Lizzies, which closed at the end of 1994. It only operated until 1997, when it was dismantled and sold due to the closure of Opryland USA.

Premier Parks bought the ride as part of a 13 ride package from the defunct park in November 1997. The ride was reported to be rebuilt and relocated to Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in Thorntown, Indiana, USA, but instead subsequently reopened at The New Marine World Theme Park in Vallejo, California, USA in May 1998 as Kong.

Alongside Boomerang Coast to Coaster, it was one of the first roller coasters at the park.

On August 5, 2016, Six Flags announced that Kong would be one of two rides to receive a virtual reality upgrade for Fright Fest, with the other one being Demon at Six Flags Great America. The ride would show a film called Rage of the Gargoyles and it was being billed as "the world's first fully immersive gaming experience". Kong would also receive some refurbished track.

Kong is a Suspended Looping Coaster installation with the 689m standard layout. Following the chain lift hill is a roll over inversion, a banked turn, a sidewinder, and a double in-line twist.

Red track and orange supports. The ride used to feature Orange track and dark green supports, with white track and green supports at Opryland
